tax: Placed at family rank, Boulder-Kin is defined by composition and formation mechanics, not by colour, size, or danger: a single welded stone body, not a bound aggregate of loose rubble. A new genus belongs here only if it shows a coherent solid-rock body with its own locomotion.
ap: The grouping is recognised by a boulder that has a low centre of mass and rights itself. A field surveyor confirms membership from at least one persistent marker — paired pressure prints or a swept rolling groove — which survives even when the body disperses.
eco: Boulder-Kin occupies granite benches and steep stone chutes. Persistence is elemental rather than reproductive: members form where fault pressure welds a settled stone mass and recur with renewed fault flex, sustaining themselves on tectonic pressure and gravity.
beh: Across the grouping, behaviour is the disturbance-response of the medium: they pose as boulders and rouse to footfall or quarrying. Roles, moods, and summoning circumstances are recorded on the entries below, not treated as new taxa.
traitsText: Shared characters: immense mass, crushing immunity, and blindness away from contact. These are grouping-level traits only; specific resistances, abilities, and field signs belong to each genus and species beneath this node.
abilities: Grouping-level abilities are the broad capacities implied by the shared body: stone-fusing repair and mass or momentum attacks. Named, bounded powers are assigned to the species that actually possess them.
bg: In the Throat, Boulder-Kin matters because they are the deadliest misidentified boulders on the worked benches. It lets survey reports sort solid-rock hazards from loose-rubble ones without collapsing distinct hazards into one vague label.
g: Boulder-Kin is the family-rank grouping of solid-rock lithic elementals that keep a single coherent stone body within the True Elementals. Its members share a welded mass of solid rock that moves as one piece, and divide below into genus groups by whether that mass walks upright or rolls. The Boulder-Kin are the heaviest true elementals a Tier III crew is likely to wake.
